content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : File                                    Date : Nov. 5, 1976


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
         Ken Finney

Subject: Telephone conversation with Mr. Whittington
          from El Dorado County


Mr. Whittington of the County Counsel's Office for El Dorado
County called November 4 to inquire about the following prob-
lem.

     A new member of a school board is the spouse of an employee
of the school district who participates in negotiations about
salaries that employees of the school district will be paid.
Mr. Whittington wondered whether the new school board member
would be prohibited from participating in negotiations and de-
cisions relative to the salary to be paid to the school em-
ployees (including a school board member's spouse). I indi-
cated that the school board member would be able to partici-
pate because the definition of income excludes salary from
a local government agency. Section 82030(b)(2). Since salary
from a local government agency is excluded from the definition
of income the school district will not be a source of income
to the school district employee and, hence, to the school
board member by reason of a community property interest in
the salary of the school district employee.

     I cautioned Mr. Whittington that my interpretation was limited
to the Political Reform Act and that I was not conversant with
other provisions of state law (such as in the Education Code)
which may have a bearing on this issue.

KF:jo

sections.question: Whether a new school board member who is the spouse of a school district employee participating in salary negotiations would be prohibited from participating in negotiations and decisions relative to the salary to be paid to school employees (including the school board member's spouse).
sections.conclusion: The school board member would be able to participate because the definition of income excludes salary from a local government agency under Section 82030(b)(2). Since salary from a local government agency is excluded from the definition of income, the school district will not be a source of income to the school district employee and, hence, to the school board member by reason of a community property interest in the salary of the school district employee.
sections.question_synthetic: May a school board member who is married to a school district employee participate in salary negotiations and decisions affecting that employee's compensation?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The school board member may participate in salary negotiations and decisions because the Political Reform Act excludes local government agency salaries from the definition of income, preventing any conflict of interest from the spouse's community property interest in those earnings.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Memorandum format documenting a telephone conversation advice. Contains clear question and conclusion. Ken Finney appropriately noted limitation to Political Reform Act only, not other state laws like Education Code.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a new school board member who is the spouse of a school district employee participating in salary negotiations would be prohibited from participating in negotiations and decisions relative to the salary to be paid to school employees (including the school board member's spouse).

CONCLUSION: The school board member would be able to participate because the definition of income excludes salary from a local government agency under Section 82030(b)(2). Since salary from a local government agency is excluded from the definition of income, the school district will not be a source of income to the school district employee and, hence, to the school board member by reason of a community property interest in the salary of the school district employee.

Mr. Whittington wondered whether the new school board member would be prohibited from participating in negotiations and de- cisions relative to the salary to be paid to the school em- ployees (including a school board member's spouse). I indi- cated that the school board member would be able to partici- pate because the definition of income excludes salary from a local government agency. Section 82030(b)(2). Since salary from a local government agency is excluded from the definition of income the school district will not be a source of income to the school district employee and, hence, to the school board member by reason of a community property interest in the salary of the school district employee. I cautioned Mr. Whittington that my interpretation was limited to the Political Reform Act and that I was not conversant with other provisions of state law (such as in the Education Code) which may have a bearing on this issue. embedding.summary: FPPC advised that a school board member spouse of a school district employee may participate in salary negotiations because local government salaries are excluded from the definition of income under the Political Reform Act.
